description:
**Problem**
We want local‑first performance visibility without SQLite. Metrics are already emitted via tracing, but there’s no explicit structured‑log contract for offline analysis.

**Design**
- Define a metrics log schema (fields + targets) and ensure JSON logging captures it (metric name, value, labels).
- For daemon runs, keep file logging JSON by default and include span data (current span + span list).
- Add a small doc (or comment in telemetry) describing the schema so tools can parse logs without guesswork.
- Verify metrics log output is preserved even when general verbosity is low (coordinate with metrics config bead).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Metrics log schema documented (field names + examples).
- [ ] JSON file logs include metrics fields + span context.
- [ ] Tests or fixtures validate at least one metrics event shape.

**Files:** src/telemetry.rs, src/daemon/metrics.rs, docs/* (if we add a doc)
title: Observability: structured metrics logs (no SQLite)

closed_reason: Completed: update --reason now supported on bd update
description: CLI update rejects --reason; update should map reason to closure when --status=closed.
title: bd update should accept --reason when closing

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Mixed-source daemon tests assert that valid checkpoint state is imported alongside watermark reuse.
- [ ] Daemon tests prove forced rebuild dirtiness is scoped to the incompatible checkpoint group.
- [ ] The new tests fail if mixed-source self-heal regresses.
description:
**Problem**
The daemon mixed-source incompatible-checkpoint tests prove watermarks and rebuild scheduling, but they do not yet prove that valid checkpoint state is actually imported when another source is incompatible, nor that rebuild dirtiness stays scoped to the incompatible checkpoint group. That leaves the self-heal contract under-proven.

**Files:** `crates/beads-daemon/src/runtime/core/mod.rs`
design:
**Design**
Extend the daemon test matrix around mixed-source checkpoint loading.

- In mixed valid/incompatible cases, assert that the surviving valid checkpoint state is merged into runtime state, not just its watermarks.
- Add a multi-group scenario that proves only the incompatible group is forced dirty while sibling groups remain clean.
- Keep runtime strictness unchanged; this is proof hardening only.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Final audit on 2026-03-11 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover @ 3f3fa295: mixed-source checkpoint self-heal coverage now proves that surviving valid checkpoint state is actually merged, not just its watermarks, and that forced rebuild dirtiness stays scoped to the incompatible checkpoint group while sibling groups remain clean. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check; just dylint; cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings; cargo test; cargo test -p beads-rs migration --features slow-tests -- --nocapture. --repo /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs

title: Mixed-source checkpoint self-heal tests should pin imported state and group isolation

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Integration child-process fixtures clear ambient config/git overrides before spawning `bd` or daemon children
- [ ] Tests keep using explicit per-test runtime/data/config roots
- [ ] Canonical fast and slow tiers pass in a shell with non-default BD/GIT env present
description:
**Problem**
Shared test helpers for spawning `bd` and daemon children inherit ambient config/git env (`BD_CONFIG_DIR`, `BD_RUNTIME_DIR`, `BD_REPL_LISTEN_ADDR`, `BD_LOG_*`, `BD_ACTOR`, `GIT_DIR`, `GIT_WORK_TREE`, `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`, etc.). That means a developer shell or wrapper process can redirect supposedly isolated tests into shared config dirs, shared sockets, or the wrong repo.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/bd_runtime.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/repl_rig.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/daemon/logging.rs`
- `crates/beads-bootstrap/src/config/*`
- `crates/beads-bootstrap/src/repo.rs`
- `crates/beads-surface/src/ipc/client.rs`
design: Centralize child-process spawn setup so tests clear the full config/git override set before applying explicit per-test values. The shared helper should scrub bootstrap config env keys plus git discovery env, set `BD_RUNTIME_DIR`/`XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` explicitly, and point config lookup at an empty per-test root.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Closed from /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs.

Landed isolation hardening:
- centralized env scrubbing in `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/bd_runtime.rs` for bootstrap config keys plus `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`, `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR`, `GIT_DIR`, and `GIT_WORK_TREE`
- `ReplRig`, logging fixtures, and CLI upgrade tests now use explicit per-test runtime/data/config roots after scrubbing inherited env
- `beads-surface` IPC client tests restore the process-global runtime-dir override via a dedicated guard
- WAL/dead/keepalive limits are now written into repo config as well as user config so env-hardening did not silently drop replication limits during daemon startup

Verification under hostile ambient env:
- `env BD_CONFIG_DIR=/tmp/codex-ambient-config BD_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/codex-ambient-runtime XDG_CONFIG_HOME=/tmp/codex-ambient-xdg GIT_DIR=/tmp/codex-ambient-git-dir GIT_WORK_TREE=/tmp/codex-ambient-git-worktree cargo xtest --no-fail-fast --status-level none --final-status-level slow` -> `42.099s`
- `env BD_CONFIG_DIR=/tmp/codex-ambient-config BD_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/codex-ambient-runtime XDG_CONFIG_HOME=/tmp/codex-ambient-xdg GIT_DIR=/tmp/codex-ambient-git-dir GIT_WORK_TREE=/tmp/codex-ambient-git-worktree cargo nextest run --profile slow --workspace --all-features --features slow-tests --no-fail-fast --status-level none --final-status-level slow` -> `41.689s`
- targeted regression checks: `cargo test -p beads-surface socket_dir_candidates -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration --features slow-tests daemon::logging -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration fixtures::bd_runtime::tests -- --nocapture`

title: Hermeticize integration child-process fixtures against ambient config and git env
